techcrunch.title: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
techcrunch.summary: We’ve compiled an overview of some of the top alternative browsers available today aiming to challenge Chrome and Safari.

techcrunch.title: Passionfroot raises $15M to expand its B2B creator marketplace to the US
techcrunch.summary: Passionfroot, a German startup building a marketplace connecting B2B creators with brands, has raised $15M in a Series A round led by Insight Partners.

techcrunch.title: Glow emerges from stealth at $1.2B valuation to challenge endpoint security in the AI era
techcrunch.summary: Glow is targeting a new class of endpoint risks created by the rapid adoption of AI agents and developer tools inside enterprises.

techcrunch.title: Synthesia’s AI training platform is moving beyond videos into live coaching
techcrunch.summary: Synthesia launched AI Roleplay Sessions, an interactive enterprise training platform where employees practice workplace conversations with AI avatars that provide feedback, scoring, and analytics to help companies measure training effectiveness.

techcrunch.title: The Anthropic-Physical Intelligence rumor roiling AI Twitter
techcrunch.summary: Anthropic and OpenAI's aggressive 2026 acquisition sprees set the stage for a weekend rumor.

techcrunch.title: Meta is testing an AI bedtime story app for people with no imagination
techcrunch.summary: At last, a tech company has found a way to outsource humanity's oldest pastime: using our imaginations.

techcrunch.title: OpenAI says Hugging Face was breached by its pre-release models
techcrunch.summary: OpenAI has come forward to claim responsibility for the Hugging Face breach, saying it was the result of internal testing gone awry.

techcrunch.title: Jack Dorsey is taking on Slack with Buzz, a group chat platform for teams and their AI agents
techcrunch.summary: Buzz is a group chat platform for the workplace that puts humans and their AI agents in the same conversation.

techcrunch.title: AI and the rise of the universal entertainment app
techcrunch.summary: Over the past decade, streaming platforms competed by dominating individual formats like music, video, podcasts, or audiobooks. Now, as AI makes it easier to create, organize, and recommend content, those distinctions are fading, pushing companies like Spotify, Netflix, YouTube, and TikTok to become all-purpose entertainment destinations instead.

techcrunch.title: Data centers expected to use 4x more electricity by 2035
techcrunch.summary: New data centers built through 2033 could consume as much electricity as India uses today.
